text: Increased plasma estradiol and prolonged estrus cycle have been studied in laboratory mammals by addition of various forms of estradiol (e.g. 17beta-estradiol; Kouki et al. 2005; Takahashi et al. 2013; Shirota et al. 2015). Studies involving dosing of laboratory mammals with various forms of estradiol (e.g. 17beta-estradiol) are supportive of the mechanism of increases in exposure to estradiol compounds causing prolonged estrus cycle. Increased estradiol has been shown to disrupt the estrus cycle by altering the hormone levels in rodents, with neonatal estrogenization disrupting signalling in the hypothalamic–pituitary-gonadal (HPG) axis leading to prolonged estrus (Takahashi et al. 2013; Shirota et al. 2015).
